Football.title: Fifa quietly bring back hated rule made famous by former England manager ahead of World Cup 2026
Football.content:
FIFA has quietly reintroduced a rule on substitutions that was hated by football fans.

National team managers are now able to make eight substitutions in a friendly.





And if the bosses of both teams agree, they can make up to 11 per game.

The rule was last in place over two decades ago but a change was made when former England boss Sven-Goran Eriksson received backlash for his management of the squad.

Eriksson changed his entire Three Lions team at half-time during a friendly against Australia at Upton Park in 2003.

Mass substitutions were banned as a result of the backlash.



The reintroduction of the substitution rule comes amid a series of rule changes made ahead of the World Cup this year.

The International Football Association Board (IFAB) announced the adjustments last week.

They include the newly-introduced use of VAR for checking corners as well as cards awarded to the wrong team and second yellow cards.

The IFAB also announced a set of new rules designed to increase the pace of games by countering tactics that can be used to timewaste.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Referees will be given the power to start a five-second countdown if they feel players are taking too long over throw-ins and goal kicks.

In addition, substitutions must be completed in no more than 10 seconds, otherwise the substitute must stay off the pitch for another minute.

Football.title: Jeremy Jacquet ‘out for season’ with major surgery after new £60m Liverpool star was left screaming in agony with injury
Football.content:
INCOMING Liverpool defender Jeremy Jacquet has been ruled out for the remainder of the season after undergoing surgery.

The £60million star agreed to join the Reds from Stade Rennais in January after a transfer battle between them and Chelsea.





Jacquet, 20, suffered a horror injury earlier this month as he left screaming in agony in the match against Lens.

The Frenchman appeared to badly hurt his shoulder and has since been having tests on the injury.

He has now been scheduled to have surgery in the coming days which could rule him out for the remainder of the season.

A club statement read: “Jeremy Jacquet is set to undergo surgery.



“On the pitch against Lens on February 7th, Jeremy Jacquet was forced to leave the game early due to a left shoulder injury.

“Following medical examinations, surgery has been scheduled in the coming days.

“Stade Rennais F.C. wishes him a speedy recovery.”

MORE TO FOLLOW…

Football.title: Ex-Newcastle star Daryl Janmaat reveals cocaine addiction destroyed his marriage and ‘caused a lot of damage’
Football.content:
DARYL JANMAAT has revealed how his cocaine addiction destroyed his marriage.

The former Newcastle star hung up his boots at Dutch side ADO Den Haag in 2022.





Janmaat, 36, starred in the Premier League for six years between 2014 and 2020.

He had spent two years at the Magpies following a transfer from Feyenoord before he moved to Watford.

After his playing days ended, he became a technical manager at ADO Den Haag.

But losing the regimental day-to-day lifestyle of a professional footballer started to take its toll.



Janmaat has revealed to Dutch media that he developed a cocaine addiction that “caused a lot of damage” to his life.

He told newspaper AD: “I have three children who also hear and read things.

“I can’t and don’t want to go into all the details, but my cocaine addiction has caused a lot of damage.

“I was supposed to get help from everyone and everything, but I was left to fend for myself. It was nothing.



“Suddenly, I lost the structure I’d had for years as a footballer. That was difficult.

“The cocaine addiction gradually crept in. You start lying to the people you love. That’s terrible; I hurt a lot of people.

“When all that disappeared and I felt completely out of place as technical manager at ADO, things went wrong. Very much so.”

Janmaat also admitted that the habit seriously affected his relationship with his wife Yoshi.

The couple had been together since 2008, but they have now parted ways despite still technically being married.



He added: “I’m still officially married, but we’re no longer together.

“The relationship wasn’t going well, you know, but the addiction obviously didn’t help.

“A lot of damage has been done, although we’re on good terms again.”



Having left his role at ADO Den Haag, Janmaat now runs his own gym business called High Power Gym in Scheveningen.

He has turned his life around despite letting things get “really bad”.

Janmaat said: “Cocaine destroys a lot. My family and friends were there for me, but I let a lot of people down.

“You start lying and distorting things. That’s exhausting, but above all, very painful.

“Was it life-threatening? Well, I was in deep, very deep. There were times when I was really, really bad, let’s just say that.



“I had everything as a player, and I still do, actually. But a lot has happened in the meantime. Too much.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Addiction is truly a battle, where you’re at your wits’ end. Literally. You’re in a struggle; it really destroys a lot.

“In a clinic like that, you have therapy and counselling. After that, I went in a different direction. Yes, the right direction. Fortunately.”

If you are affected by any of the issues raised in this article, please call the Samaritans for free on 116123.

Football.title: Lynda Hale dead at 72: Pioneering England women’s footballer who scored in Lionesses’ first ever game passes away
Football.content:
FORMER England winger Lynda Hale has passed away at the age of 72.

Hale made history in November 1972 by playing in the first-ever England Women’s match against Scotland.





The Southampton star, who was only 18 at the time, scored as the Lionesses won 3-2 at Ravenscraig Park.

She was later allocated the No7 in the nation’s Leagacy Numbers.

Reflecting on her life and career, FA chair Debbie Hewitt said: “I am so deeply saddened to learn of Lynda Hale’s passing.

“Whenever we talk about the success of our Lionesses in recent years, it is always with rightful recognition of those special players who paved the way.



“Our first ever No7 and a goalscorer in the first official match for England women, Lynda will always be remembered for the part she played in our football history.

“On behalf of everyone at The FA and all connected to the Lionesses, our thoughts are with her family and friends.”

England will wear black armbands in their opening World Cup qualifier against Ukraine on Tuesday night in memory of Hale.

The winger was also a seven-time Women’s FA Cup winner with Southampton Women, an independent side unaffiliated to the WSL2 outfit.



The club will honour her at their home game against Wycombe on Sunday.

Chairman Aaron Smith said: “This heartbreaking news has hit our club and community hard. Lynda was one of the pioneers of women’s football, a player and person that helped create what it is today.

“Representing our wonderful club and England, she has brought joy to the area.”

Football.title: Former Premier League wag puts on leggy display in Japanese-inspired outfit as fans label her ‘amazingly gorgeous’
Football.content:
A FORMER Premier League Wag has stunned fans with a leggy display on social media.

Viktoria Varga, once dubbed the “world’s most beautiful woman,” was married to former Southampton star Graziano Pelle.





The Italian footballer and Hungarian model were together for ten years before their divorce in 2022.

The split has not stopped Viktoria, 33, from showing off her glamorous lifestyle to her 618,000 Instagram followers.

She regularly shares snaps from high-fashion photoshoots and shows off her stunning outfits.

In a recent post, the blonde bombshell left fans speechless in a Japanese-inspired outfit ahead of a visit to celebrity haunt Nobu.

Fans flooded the comment section with praise for the leggy display.

One fan posted: “Amazingly gorgeous and always got that beautiful smile.”

A second wrote: “Beautiful, stylish and elegant.”

Another admirer added: “Always beautiful.”



While one added: “I say this all the time… you are unique.”

Viktoria’s ex-fella Pelle, 40, enjoyed a memorable spell at the Saints where he bagged 30 goals in 80 games.



The Italian arrived on the South Coast after a prolific stint at Feyenoord, where he netted 50 goals in just 59 outings.

After leaving Southampton in 2016, Pelle spent five years in China with Shandong Luneng before returning to Italy to hang up his boots.

Football.title: Cristiano Ronaldo’s £61m private jet leaves Saudi for Madrid after Iranian blitz on US embassy in Riyadh
Football.content:
CRISTIANO Ronaldo’s luxury £61million private jet left Saudi Arabia in the middle of the night as bombs rained down on the US embassy in Riyadh.

Ronaldo, 41, lives in the Saudi capital with his partner, Georgina Rodriguez, and his five children, but it is so far unclear whether they were aboard the plane.





Two drone strikes targeted the US diplomatic headquarters in the country overnight, according to Iranian state media.

The rogue state also announced that a staff building in Bahrain had been destroyed.

Foreign nationals are fleeing the Middle East in their thousands as the bloody conflict enters its fourth day.



It comes as…

It comes as Ronaldo’s old Manchester United teammate Rio Ferdinand admitted he was “scared” as “frightening” missile strikes forced his family into a Covid-like lockdown in Dubai.

A flight tracker showed that the football superstar’s Bombardier Global Express jet made the near seven-hour journey to the Spanish capital.



The luxury aircraft departed at 8pm and touched down at almost 1am.

Flightradar24 shows the Portuguese legend’s jet flew over Egypt and across the Mediterranean before landing in Madrid.

Ronaldo splashed out an eye-watering £61million on the state-of-the-art jet in 2024 after selling his Gulfstream G200, which he bought for £16million in 2015.

The sleek all-black jet can hold 15 passengers and features a number of different rooms, including a suite with a double bed and a separate shower area.

It is adorned with the footballer’s logo throughout and comes complete with a silhouette of his iconic celebration.



Rodriguez used the plane for a trip to Milan just last week and shared a snap of herself wearing a black bra on the journey.

The Sun has reached out to Ronaldo’s representation for comment.

Ronaldo’s old friend Ferdinand, 41, has been forced to take cover in his Dubai basement amid the waves of Iranian strikes.



The Brit lives in the UAE with wife Kate, 34, and their children Cree, five, and Shae, two, plus Rio’s daughter Tia, 14.

His sons Lorenz, 19, and Tate, 17, did not move over when they relocated from the UK last year to focus on their football careers.

US and Israeli bombings on Iran prompted retaliatory hits on various targets around the region – and that includes the UAE.

And Ferdinand explained just how fearful he is.

Speaking on his show Rio Ferdinand Presents, the former Manchester United and England defender said: “Been a different week for me, not going to lie.



“Firstly, I’m in great condition this morning as the whole family did a workout.

“A bit like in Covid, the whole family is doing things we wouldn’t usually be doing together.

“Home-schooling a bit today.

“It’s frightening when you hear missiles, planes and fighter jets – I don’t know what it is – going above us, and you’re hearing big bombs, and what that is we don’t know as we don’t know the details of what they are.

“It’s telling your kids what it is and helping them navigate through this moment, which is important especially as the dad of the house.



“You want to try and remain calm and keep everybody as calm as possible.”

Ferdinand added: “I’ve got to be honest guys, it’s been a bit of a scary situation.

“But at the same time, weirdly enough, I’ve felt very secure and looked after. My studio has become my bunker.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We were advised to go into the basement on the first night that all the noise started and we were down here sleeping with duvets and stuff.



“We are utilising the space wisely.”

Football.title: Macclesfield stadium set on fire with emergency services called to scene as club warn fans to stay away
Football.content:
MACCLESFIELD have told locals to stay away from their stadium after it was set on fire.

A club statement released in the early hours of Tuesday morning urged fans to “avoid the area” following a suspected arson attempt at Moss Rose, known as the Leasing.com Stadium for sponsorship reasons.





The National League North side said emergency services had responded and were on site tackling the blaze, which had been started either late on Monday night or early on Tuesday.

A statement from Cheshire Fire & Rescue Service said two engines from Macclesfield had attended the scene, where flames were visible over a wall of the Moss Rose stadium.

They confirmed an outbuilding adjacent to the club’s shop was alight.

The service confirmed two firefighters wearing breathing apparatus tackled the blaze using hose reel water jets, and remained at the scene until 4:30am.



They confirmed: “An initial investigation indicates that the fire was started deliberately.”

Fans were asked to avoid the area and local residents told to keep windows shut and to not approach the stadium while emergency services were working.

The Silkmen statement said: “We would like to reassure everyone the situation is under control.

“There are no casualties and investigations are already underway in to the cause of the fire.



“We would like to thank everyone for their concern and the timely response of those who alerted emergency services so quickly.”

Cheshire Police also attended the incident.

Macclesfield are due to travel to play Darlington tonight in the National League North, and a statement to SunSport confirmed the team would travel to the clash as planned.

They last played at home on February 24, a 4-1 defeat to Fylde.

The ground hosted Premier League sides Crystal Palace and Brentford in the FA Cup this season.



Macclesfield caused a huge upset by beating the Eagles 2-1 in the third round, but were narrowly beaten by the Bees in round four last month.

Football.title: Kai Rooney's true colours clear for all to see as Coleen speaks out about son at Man Utd
Football.content:
Kai Rooney's true colours clear for all to see as Coleen speaks out about son at Man Utd

Coleen Rooney has spoken about Kai's attitude to football fame and constant comparisons to Wayne

View 3 Images Kai Rooney is making waves at Manchester United (Image: Anthony Devlin, Getty Images for Disney+)

Coleen Rooney has revealed how her footballer son, Kai, deals with being the son of Manchester United great Wayne. Kai and his younger brother Klay are both part of United's academy.

‌



At 16, Kai is the age Wayne was when he made his stunning first-team debut at Everton in 2003. While he isn't quite ready to make the leap to the Premier League, he is getting closer to the United first-team squad, having recently graduated to the under-18s, managed by Darren Fletcher.

‌



Earlier this season, he made his playing debut at Old Trafford in the FA Youth Cup. Naturally, he is often asked about his ability compared to Wayne's. Far from being annoyed by the persistent question, Coleen says he takes it all in his stride.

‌



She told The Times: "He's lived it from a young age. When we used to go to matches when he was younger, he'd get a lot of people coming up saying, 'Oh, are you Wayne Rooney's son?' But he's strong-minded, always has been. I've got no worries in the way he deals with it."

Having been together since childhood, the parents at least know what to expect if Kai makes the big time. Coleen said: "It's nice that we know what to expect.

‌



"And we can try to guide him. Obviously, it's his life, his passion. He can decide what he wants to do. But I think it's good we've got the knowledge."

View 3 Images Kai Rooney is climbing the ranks at Old Trafford (Image: Anthony Devlin/Manchester United via Getty Images)

In a previous interview with The Mirror, Coleen revealed what Kai tells people when they ask him about Wayne. She said: "I think they sometimes get pressure off other people, you know.

‌



"People approach them now and then if we're out, they do say, 'Are you are as good as your dad?' Kai says, 'I'm a different type of player.' You've got to let them know that you don't have to follow and to be your own person."

We've always just let them decide what they want to do. My eight-year-old was playing and doing really well, but then decided he didn't want to, and that's OK."

Football.title: England players voice concerns as political issues hang over 2026 World Cup
Football.content:
England players voice concerns as political issues hang over 2026 World Cup

England will head to the World Cup this summer with the tournament littered with political issues - and players have made it clear they don't want to be rolled out as spokespeople

View 2 Images England star Harry Kane has had to face questions on non-footballing issues in the past (Image: UEFA via Getty Images)

England players have made it clear they don't want to get caught up in any political issues when it comes to this summer's World Cup in North America.

‌



The tournament is again attracting attention for issues that don't necessarily involve football. It has created moral dilemmas for certain countries, who want to be present but could be made to take a stand. Groups have raised issues with Donald Trump's travel bans, immigration crackdowns whilst the military action launched against Iran and Venezuela has cast doubt over their involvement.

‌



Leading figures from each team, including England, have previously been asked to comment on topics that don't relate to football. It has put them in difficult positions with a country's association often taking a lead on major decisions.

‌



And PFA chief Maheta Molango has admitted that players - including Harry Kane - have come to him and made it clear they don't want to be ploughed out as spokespeople for their countries, which has been the case previously.

Spain vs Argentina ‘Finalissima’ clash under threat as Qatar FA take drastic action READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

He told Sky News: "We're talking about people who are smart, who are socially conscious, people who understand that they don't live in a bubble, people will really understand that they have a platform, that platform can be used to create a better world and to try to influence people.

‌



"However, what some of them have told me is that they found it a little bit unfair that ultimately, for as much as they have a platform, why should they turn into spokespeople for governments or for governing bodies that in reality should be the ones taking the lead? I think some of them felt that in the past, and we've seen that, for example, in Qatar, some of them basically were hung out to dry."

Molango added that "the players were asked to become political spokespeople and that's not what they are" as he called on Government officials to be the ones fielding such questions - not placing the heat elsewhere.

View 2 Images The World Cup in North America is attracting a lot of political attention (Image: FIFA via Getty Images)

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Several leading England stars, as well as then boss Gareth Southgate, were forced to talk about political issues at the last World Cup, which was hosted by Qatar. Their human rights record and their attitude towards the LGBTQ community were both criticised.

England were asked whether their skipper would do a rainbow coloured armband amid the threat of a booking. Years earlier at the delayed Euro 2020, which was largely played on home soil, the teams' decision to consistently take the knee saw them clash with Members of Parliament.

Football.title: Rio Ferdinand shares new family update after describing 'missiles and big bombs' in Dubai
Football.content:
Rio Ferdinand shares new family update after describing 'missiles and big bombs' in Dubai

Rio Ferdinand, his wife Kate and their children have been sharing what life is like for them in Dubai during terrifying missile strikes and drone attacks by Iran

View 4 Images Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate live in Dubai with their children (Image: xkateferdinand/Instagram)

Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate have given a glimpse into their life in Dubai during the "scary" current situation. The city in the United Arab Emirates has been attacked by missiles and drones from Iran in recent days.

‌



The former England and Manchester United defender moved with his family to Dubai in August. The 47-year-old lives in the Middle Eastern city with Kate, 34, their children Shae, five and Cree, two, and Rio's daughter Tia, 14.

‌



Their usual lifestyle has been turned on its head in recent days after Israel and the United States launched strikes on Iran, sparking retaliation attacks. Iran has responded by targeting nearby countries Bahrain, Qatar, the UAE, Kuwait, Jordan and Oman.

‌



Iran has attacked Dubai, damaging Dubai International Airport and landmarks like the Fairmont The Palm hotel in the Palm Jumeirah area and the Burj Al Arab hotel. Speaking on his podcast on Monday, Ferdinand opened up on what it has been like in recent days for his family, describing their "scary" situation.

England cricket star Jonny Bairstow worried for family's safety in Dubai after missile attacks READ MORE :

And now he has shared a photo taken by Kate in the gym, with the caption: "Good morning. A very hectic but wholesome family workout this morning. Hope everyone is ok."

Ferdinand went into detail about what the last few days have entailed, revealing the family took shelter in the basement for safety. "Been a different week for me, not going to lie," he said on Rio Ferdinand Presents.

‌



"Firstly, I'm in great condition this morning as the whole family did a workout. A bit like in Covid, the whole family is doing things we wouldn't usually be doing together.

4 View 4 Images Kate Ferdinand shared a photo of herself in the gym with her children (Image: Instagram/@xkateferdinand) 4 View 4 Images Rio and Kate Ferdinand in Dubai (Image: xkateferdinand/Instagram)

‌



"Home-schooling a bit today. It's frightening when you hear missiles, planes and fighter jets - I don't know what it is - going above us, and you're hearing big bombs, and what that is we don't know as we don't know the details of what they are.

"It's telling your kids what it is and helping them navigate through this moment, which is important especially as the dad of the house. You want to try and remain calm and keep everybody as calm as possible.

View 4 Images Ferdinand moved his young family to Dubai in August 2025 (Image: rioferdy5/Instagram)

Article continues below

"I've got to be honest guys, it's been a bit of a scary situation but at the same time weirdly enough I've felt very secure and looked after. My studio has become my bunker.

"We were advised to go into the basement on the first night of all the noise started and we were down here sleeping with duvets and stuff. We are utilising the space wisely."

The UK government's advice for the UAE currently reads: "Due to reported missile attacks, British nationals in UAE should immediately shelter in place. Remain indoors in a secure location, avoid all travel and follow instructions from the local authorities."

Football.title: Roy Keane accused of being a 'bully and hypocrite' by ex-Man Utd masseuse
Football.content:
Roy Keane accused of being a 'bully and hypocrite' by ex-Man Utd masseuse

Roy Keane has been on the receiving end of an attack as a former Manchester United staff member calls out his bullying in retaliation to comments made by the Irishman

View 2 Images Roy Keane first made his views known on an episode of the Overlap (Image: Stick to Football)

Roy Keane has been branded a "bully" by a former Manchester United physio after the Irishman claimed that they were "scavengers in the dressing room.”

‌



The former Red Devils captain took aim at some of the club's staff, where he accused them of trying to "run the show". But Rod Thornley, who worked for United for 19 years under Sir Alex Ferguson, has suggested that the Irishman's rant was a personal attack aimed at him after the pair fell out.

‌



Keane is still renowned for his old school values and throughout his time as captain in Manchester was known for keeping standards high. He also wouldn't shy away from speaking his mind if he felt it was in the team's best interests.

‌



But he seemed to have little time for the physios or the masseuses as he claimed "they’d get a bit cocky", accusing them of trying to buy players' cars on the cheap and "wearing their headphones as if they were players".

Thornley, who left United in 2019, hit back as he told The Busby Way podcast: “I witnessed him (Keane) bully people many a time.

Man Utd youngster Chido Obi forced off in U21 clash with worrying head injury READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



“He went on about masseuses and buying players’ cars. He sold his car to the caretaker at Man United. So like, I can’t buy one off a player, but you can sell it to a member of staff.”

He blasted: “All the things he said were just bulls***, just aimed trying to hurt me. I then got abused online massively and it affected me mentally. It just was f***ing horrible. It’s typical him – bullying someone. That’s what Roy Keane's about a lot of the times.”

Thornley also labelled Keane a "hypocrite" for allowing his daughter Caragh on to The Overlap to promote her breakfast cereal brand.

‌



View 2 Images Rod Thornley eventually left the club in 2019 (Image: Getty)

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Keane aired his frustrations with and masseuses way back in June 2024 when he said: “What used to annoy me was the lads who used to do the massages, they’d get a bit cocky. They settle into the club then they think they’re running the show, have got the music on and the team wins something, and all the masseurs would be on the pitch.

‌



“We’re having a night out, free bar, and then they’d come with all their mates. Then they’d be wearing their headphones as if they were players, coming off the bus. Anyone selling their car?’ – they’d try and buy the players’ cars on the cheap.

“They hang around and get players’ boots. They end up having loads of boots and the lads’ trainers – scavengers in the dressing room.”

Football.title: Paul Scholes wants Man Utd to make offer to international boss immediately after World Cup
Football.content:
Paul Scholes wants Man Utd to make offer to international boss immediately after World Cup

Manchester United icon Paul Scholes has put forward the manager he believes the club should target after the World Cup

View 2 Images Paul Scholes thinks Carlo Ancelotti is 'perfect' for Man United (Image: Getty Images)

Paul Scholes has suggested that Manchester United should look to appoint Carlo Ancelotti as their next manager this summer. While the club is expected to make a permanent appointment at the end of the season, interim head coach Michael Carrick has impressed since taking over from Ruben Amorim in January and could be in contention to take the job on a long‑term basis.

‌



Former midfielder Carrick now has the joint‑best record of any Premier League manager across their first seven games, winning six and drawing one. United's 2-1 comeback against Crystal Palace strengthened their hold on a top‑five place, lifting them above Aston Villa and into third.

‌



However, Scholes believes Ancelotti is the ideal manager for the club. Speaking on The Good, The Bad & The Football Podcast, he said: "The perfect one out there - I say perfect - Ancelotti is still out there. I know he is coach of Brazil.

‌



Jurgen Klopp's thoughts on managing Man Utd after meeting and contract put in front of him READ MORE :

Diogo Dalot fires Champions League warning to Man Utd teammates after red-hot run of form READ MORE :

"He is the perfect one for Manchester United where you think he will make players feel a million dollars. And he has the experience of winning stuff.

View 2 Images Carlo Ancelotti will lead Brazil at this summer's World Cup in North America (Image: Getty Images)

"But the question is, is he too old now? That's the only thing. Is there anyone else of that ilk, with more experience? They need presence as a leader."

‌



Ancelotti managed Real Madrid for four years before leaving the club last year, and while he will lead Brazil at this summer’s World Cup, he has been out of domestic football since his departure.

Scholes, meanwhile, explained why he believes Carrick may not yet have the experience required to lead the Red Devils forward.

"The question is, has Michael got the experience?" Scholes continued. "I know it's a big question, I know he has managed in the Championship, but this is different.

‌



"Can we see Michael winning the Premier League? I don't know yet, and I think the biggest thing is, what type of manager will they look for? A tactician or a man manager who looks after people? Ole Gunnar [Solskjaer] was that.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

"But all the people who are man managers, they always have a brilliant coach next to them, which Michael has got [Steve Holland].

‌



"The club have to decide, do they want a master tactician, which has failed. Ole Gunnar had the man management skills, but did he have the experience to go on and win a trophy?

"The same questions will be asked of Michael."

United have ten games left this season to cement their place in the top four as they push to return to European competition.

‌



United's last Champions League appearance came in the 2023/24 campaign, when they finished bottom of their group and exited early. The following season, they reached the Europa League final, where they were beaten 1-0 by Tottenham in Bilbao.

Football.title: Cristiano Ronaldo's £61m private jet leaves Saudi Arabia as Iran conflict escalates
Football.content:
Cristiano Ronaldo's £61m private jet leaves Saudi Arabia as Iran conflict escalates

Cristiano Ronaldo's private jet left Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, where he plays for Al-Nassr, yesterday night, with flight tracking data showing it landed in Madrid in the early hours

View 5 Images Cristiano Ronaldo's private jet left Riyadh for Madrid (Image: Manchester Airport)

Cristiano Ronaldo's private jet left Riyadh for Madrid on Monday night following the escalation of tensions in the Middle East.

‌



Thousands of people are fleeing the region as Iran launches retaliation following attacks by Israel and the United States and it appears as though Ronaldo might be one of them.

‌



Flight tracking data showed that Ronaldo's £61million jet left Saudi Arabia on Monday night at around 8pm and landed in the Spanish capital, where he previously played for Real Madrid, at 1am. Flightradar24 showed it travelled west over Egypt and the Mediterranean before landing in Spain.

‌



Commercial flights in the region have been grounded due to conflict, but the 41-year-old, who plays for Al-Nassr in the Saudi capital, owns a Bombadier Global Express 6500 jet. He has lived in Riyadh since joining Al-Nassr from Manchester United in December 2022, where he earns £488,000 per day.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Cristiano Ronaldo misses penalty as unwanted five year run ends with Al Nassr READ MORE :

He is currently injured, having gone off in Al Nassr's 3-1 win over Al Fahya on Saturday, so isn't expected to play their next match. Al-Nassr's Asian Champions League quarter-final at Al-Wasl, that was scheduled for Wednesday, was postponed on Monday due to the conflict.

‌



Iran has targeted Saudi Arabia in recent days, with the country's Ministry of Defense confirming on Tuesday morning that the US embassy in Riyadh had been attacked by two drones. The attack ended "in a limited fire and minor material damage to the building".

It came after Iran struck Saudi Arabia's Ras Tanura refinery, the world's largest oil refinery, causing a spike in worldwide oil prices.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



5 View 5 Images Cristiano Ronaldo on his private jet (Image: INSTAGRAM@https://www.instagram.com/jacobandco/?hl=en) 5 View 5 Images The jet has luxury fittings (Image: Cristiano/Instagram)

Two years ago, Ronaldo sold his Gulfstream G200 private jet, that he purchased for £16m in 2015, and upgraded to the Bombadier Global Express 6500. It can carry 15 passengers and has a suite with a double bed and a separate shower.

‌



His fiancé Georgina Rodriguez recently used the jet to travel to Milan, sharing photos of her journey with her 72 million followers on Instagram. The couple, who originally met in Madrid, live with Ronaldo's five children in Riyadh.

The US Mission to Saudi Arabia has issued a shelter in place notification for Jeddah, Riyadh and Dhahran. Meanwhile, football in Saudi Arabia has been put on hold.

5 View 5 Images Georgina Rodriguez on the private jet 5 View 5 Images Cristiano Ronaldo has played in Saudi Arabia for three years (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"In light of the developing situation in the Middle East, the Asian Football Confederation has confirmed that the AFC Champions League Elite 2025/26 Round of 16 first-leg matches in the West Region, originally scheduled for March 2-3, 2026, will now be rescheduled," a statement from the Asian Cup organisers read on Monday.

"Additionally, the AFC Champions League Two and AFC Challenge League quarter-final first-leg matches involving teams in the West Region, originally scheduled to be played between March 3 and 4, 2026, are also postponed until further notice.

"Matches involving clubs from the East Region across all of the AFC's club competitions will proceed as originally scheduled. The AFC will continue to closely monitor this rapidly evolving situation and remains resolute in ensuring the safety and security of all players, teams, officials, and fans."

Football.title: Antonio Rudiger avoids punishment despite footage showing knee to the face
Football.content:
Antonio Rudiger avoids punishment despite footage showing knee to the face

Real Madrid were stunned by Getafe at the Bernabeu but defender Antonio Rudiger dodged any punishment for a knee to the face of his opponent - which VAR also checked

View 2 Images Antonio Rudiger planted his knee into his opponent's face

Antonio Rudiger escaped any punishment during Real Madrid's loss to Getafe despite kneeing an opponent in the face.

‌



VAR had a look at the German's questionable challenge but deemed it sufficient and the referee was not urged to visit the monitor for a second look. Diego Rico had already been scythed down by a tackle and was on the ground when Rudiger came in with a second challenge.

‌



The ex-Chelsea defender dropped and led with his knee, which struck Rico in the face. Despite the nasty looking challenge he didn't even receive a yellow card, which left Getafe boss Jose Bordalas stunned.

‌



He had masterminded a win at the Bernabeu, but refused to be drawn on the challenge, although his feelings appeared crystal clear. He said: "Everyone saw it. But I won't talk about it. What we have to do is enjoy the victory."

Tempers did boil over in stoppage time as two red cards were shown. Both sets of players clashed on the touchline late on with Madrid star Franco Mastantuono sent off in the 95th minute for dissent. Adrian Liso was also dismissed after picking up a second yellow in the seventh minute of added time.

'Angry' Kylian Mbappe suffers huge Real Madrid blow ahead of Man City showdown READ MORE :

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



Real were visibly frustrated as the defeat saw them fall four points behind Barcelona, who had claimed victory on Saturday. Martin Satriano's spectacular strike resulted in a shock win for Getafe, who leapt up to 11th spot.

Los Blancos are currently without star forward Kylian Mbappe, who has a knee sprain, and Englishman Jude Bellingham as he continues to nurse a thigh problem. Their absence proved telling but Alvaro Arbeloa refused to admit defeat in the race for the La Liga title.

View 2 Images VAR checked the challenge but didn't call for any punishment

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Real have lost successive matches but their head coach said: "There are 36 points left, and we have to fight for them. Nobody here will give up, this is Real Madrid. Four points is a gap we think we can bridge."

Arbeloa added: "We have great players, a great squad, and players who will recover and help us a lot. We can do things better on the pitch. I understand the criticism after a defeat like this.

Article continues below

"We created chances to score. Can we play better? That's also true. I can't criticise the effort of the players. It's my job to improve the play of the team."

Football.title: Macclesfield FC stadium set on fire as club issue statement to 'stay away' from area
Football.content:
Macclesfield FC have urged locals to stay away from their stadium after it was set on fire. The National League North side have confirmed there was a suspected arson attempt at their home ground.

The incident is understood to have taken place in the early hours of this morning, with footage of the spine-chilling flames being uploaded in a video on social media. The club have confirmed that no casualties or injuries as a result of the fire have been reported, with the situation now under control after emergency services were called.

As of right now, it's unclear how much damage has been caused by the blaze. The club has insisted that emergency services are carrying out an investigation into the incident, which will also assess the amount of damage that has been caused.

The club released a statement which said: "Macclesfield FC are aware of a suspected arson attempt at the Leasing.com Stadium in the late hours of yesterday evening/early hours of this morning.

"Emergency services have responded immediately and are on sit tackling the blaze in the affected areas. Despite understandable concern, we urge everyone to please avoid the area. Anyone nearby to keep windows shut and not to approach the stadium or disrupt the progress of the emergency services.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"We would like to reassure everyone that the situation is under control. There are no casualties and investigations are already underway in to the cause of the fire.

"We would like to thank everyone for their concern and the timely response of those who alerted emergency services so quickly. Macclesfield FC will be making no further comment at this stage whilst the investigation develops."

There will be more to follow on this breaking news story and Mirror Sport will bring you the very latest updates, pictures and video as soon as possible.

It has been less than a fortnight since Macclesfield last played at their home ground. They were defeated 4-1 at home by Fylde on February 24 in a match which attracted 2,790 spectators.

On February 16, they hosted Premier League side Brentford in the 4th round of the FA Cup in an exciting contest. They pushed Keith Andrews' side all the way before losing 1-0 as a result of an own goal from Sam Heathcote.

Image: AFP or licensors) AFP or licensors)

Before their defeat to the Bees, they caused the biggest upset in the FA Cup this season by beating holders Crystal Palace 2-1 in January. Their heroics in the historic competition saw them earn around £1m in extra income, which was made up of prize money as well as bonuses for being broadcast on live TV.

Macclesfield have had a fast ascent up the football pyramid ever since the club was formed as a phoenix side from the wound-up Macclesfield Town in 2020. They have already enjoyed three promotions since their formation and have had an eye on trying to earn a place in the play-offs this season.

Video Loading Video Unavailable Click to play Tap to play The video will auto-play soon 8 Cancel Play now

Chairman and co-owner Rob Smethurst also revealed the club had been in talks with two wealthy foreign groups about a potential investment in the club in the build-up to their clash against Brentford. He told City AM: "We’re in talks very recently. I can’t disclose that yet, but we’ve got the potential of a very, very big opportunity that we’re now looking at.

"The next 30 days, fingers crossed, if that does come off, then we’ll be in an unbelievable position. They’re huge organisations. They could buy Premier League clubs. We’re in talks with two. They’re actually foreign investors. But that’s all I can say."

Football.title: Lord Allen resigns as BHA chair after six months in role
Football.content:
The British Horseracing Authority has announced that Lord Allen has resigned as the ruling body’s chair after less than a year in the post.

Lord Charles Allen was nominated for the position late in 2024 after a search and he officially took up the role last September.

In a statement confirming his resignation, Lord Allen said: "Horseracing is an amazing sport with great potential and over the last year I have met some incredibly passionate people who love the sport and who believe to survive and prosper that change is needed.

"I also want to pay tribute to the hardworking and dedicated BHA team. I wish the sport well for the future."

Lord Allen took the role after the member organisations of the BHA (Racecourse Association, Racehorse Owners Association, Thoroughbred Breeders' Association and licensed personnel) unanimously agreed that there should be a completely independent board and that the BHA should have a commercial remit.

The members, who currently nominate representatives to the board, have not been able to reach agreement between themselves on changing the rules of the BHA to allow the achievement of those two goals as a change requires unanimous support. Accordingly, Lord Allen has resigned.

David Jones, the senior independent director of the BHA, said: "The board would like to thank Charles for the time and effort that he has put into the role helping us to develop a vision that is aspirational and achievable, and we regret that we were unable to put in place at this time the governance changes that would have enabled us to progress this. I have enjoyed working with him and wish him continued success."

The BHA board will discuss and determine what interim arrangements will be put in place.

Football.title: Brook: England ready for 'pressure' India semi-final after 'niggly' wins
Football.content:
Captain Harry Brook feels England coming through "niggly" games earlier in the T20 World Cup will stand them in good stead for their blockbuster semi-final against host nation India in Mumbai on Thursday.

Captivate Captivate , which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enable Captivate cookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the This content is provided by, which may be using cookies and other technologies. To show you this content, we need your permission to use cookies. You can use the buttons below to amend your preferences to enablecookies or to allow those cookies just once. You can change your settings at any time via the Privacy Options Unfortunately we have been unable to verify if you have consented to Captivate cookies. To view this content you can use the button below to allow Captivate cookies for this session only. Enable Cookies Allow Cookies Once

Brook's side stuttered to wins over Associate nations Nepal, Scotland and Italy in the first round around a meek defeat to fellow two-time champions West Indies.

They improved in the Super 8s while going undefeated, beating Sri Lanka, Pakistan - as the skipper struck a stunning 50-ball century - and then finally New Zealand having looked up against it needing 43 from the final 18 deliveries.

England's MVP of the tournament Will Jacks - who has won four Player of the Match awards - and T20 World Cup debutant Rehan Ahmed slammed an unbroken, match-winning stand of 44 from 16 balls against the Black Caps after opening pair Jos Buttler and Phil Salt struggled again, scoring a duck and two respectively.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Full highlights from Colombo as Will Jacks and Rehan Ahmed powered England to a four-wicket win over New Zealand in the T20 World Cup

I always look at T20 cricket as thinking about the reward rather than the risk. It’s a case of backing yourself and thinking about what good can come of it rather than what bad can come of it. I will never tell anyone off for taking the aggressive option – I don’t think I have a leg to stand on there!

Brook told Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward: "We are managing to scrape along in close games and that is the sign of a good side.

"I have learned you are never out of games. I know I have players with a lot of character, a willingness to win, wanting to win a lot, but also that calmness under pressure."

'If one of our batters comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads'

Buttler is averaging just 8.85 in the competition after five successive single-figure scores, while Salt's only knock of note was 62 in the Super 8s encounter with Sri Lanka.

However, Brook feels his top-order duo can fire versus India, a side England are meeting for the third straight T20 World Cup semi-final, after hammering them in Adelaide in 2022 before being thrashed by them in Guyana in 2024.

Image: Jos Buttler's scores during his T20 World Cup campaign so far

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Former England all-rounder Moeen Ali is backing Buttler to rediscover best form after yet another disappointing innings, against New Zealand

The Yorkshireman added: "We are all really excited. I have already heard a few lads say they wanted to play India anyway. It is going to be a massive occasion with a hell of a lot of pressure for both sides.

"India obviously have a hell of a lot of support and I'm not sure there are going to be many people behind us. It's going to be loud. Hopefully we can play our best cricket.

"I am excited for the lads up top. I feel they are one step closer to taking the game away from a side. I don't think there is any weakness in the top order at all. The lads have just struggled a little bit and not got the runs they always get.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Take a look at the best bits from Brook's 50-ball century against Pakistan at the T20 World Cup, a knock that featured 10 fours and four sixes

"I think Jos should be left alone. He is one of the best players to have ever played the game and I can see him coming good.

"It's awesome other lads have been able to step up. There have been performances throughout. We have so many powerful players in the top seven that if one comes off, the opposition are scratching their heads and don't really know where to go."

Jacks crucial for England after 'thanks-for-coming game'

Jacks has been instrumental for England, contributing 191 runs at No 7, including across the nerve-jangling wins over Nepal, Italy, Sri Lanka and New Zealand, as well as seven wickets with his off-spin.

Brook said of the Surrey all-rounder: "Jacks has been outstanding. To do that in his first proper World Cup playing every game is phenomenal.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player The best of Jacks' maiden T20 international fifty, an innings that rescued England against Italy and helped the team into the World Cup Super 8s

"We chatted to him in Sri Lanka [during the pre-World Cup tour] when he had a thanks-for-coming game. He didn't bowl, didn't bat and got the ball once or twice in the field.

"We said to him it was a role that gives you a lot of reward when you do win games and that you are playing this role or it is hard for you to get in the side.

"He has embraced that position and done exceptionally well. The character he has shown has been awesome."

'I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times'

Brook's ice-cool captaincy has also been a key component of England reaching their fifth straight T20 World Cup semi-final, so how has he tried to operate?

"We have had a lot of pressure games and the calmer you can stay as a captain helps in every facet of the game.

"You can see me holding on to the ball a bit longer. I want to have a conversation with the bowler, make sure they know what they are trying to bowl, make them think about one thing.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Brook gives Sky Sports Cricket's Ian Ward an insight into how he leads as a captain

"For example, with wide yorkers, I am not that bothered about the line you bowl. I just want you to hit the yorker length.

"Unless the batter is scooping, no one is going to hit you for four or six. You can set funky fields but if you nail your length it's usually one, dot or wicket.

"With us bowling a lot more spin now, we do try to get ahead of the over rate and slow the game down. It works better in certain situations than others but that is the beauty of playing spin. You can use the maximum amount of minutes you have got.

"I aways have options in my head. I tell the lads who is potentially going to be bowling the second over. Sometimes it changes, sometimes it doesn't. After that it's watch the game, see how it's playing out and try to get the most effective bowler in a situation.

"I want to make it as uncomfortable as possible for the batter coming in at all times."

Watch England vs India in the T20 World Cup semi-finals, at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, live on Sky Sports Cricket and Sky Sports Main Event from 1pm on Thursday (1.30pm first ball).

Football.title: Frustrated Rahm explains why he won't sign DP World Tour deal
Football.content:
Jon Rahm has accused the DP World Tour of "extorting players" and says he will not sign a deal that allows him to play on the circuit and in LIV Golf simultaneously under current conditions.

Unlike his Ryder Cup team-mate Tyrrell Hatton, Rahm was not among the eight DP World Tour members recently given conditional releases to appear in LIV Golf without facing fines.

The Spaniard has previously said he has "no intention" of paying the financial punishments for breaching membership guidelines by featuring in LIV events without permission, with his fines believed to be in the region of £2.5m.

Unless an agreement is reached, his eligibility for next year's Ryder Cup in Ireland could be in doubt.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rich Beem explains the DP World Tour statement on eight members to have been granted permission to play in conflicting tournaments on the LIV Golf circuit in 2026 - but why not John Rahm?

The chief sticking point for Rahm is the number of DP World Tour events he has been asked to appear in.

'I don't like the situation and I'm not going to agree to it'

Two-time major champion and former world No 1 Rahm said ahead of LIV Golf Hong Kong from Thursday: "I don't like what [the DP World Tour] are doing currently with the contract they're having us sign.

"They're asking me to play a minimum of six events, and they dictate where two of those have to be, amongst other things that I don't agree with.

"I did tell them to lower that to four events, like the minimum says, and I'll sign tonight. They haven't agreed to that. I just refuse to play six events. I don't want to and that's not what the rules say.

Image: Rahm says he would 'gladly' pay to play in next year's Ryder Cup in Ireland

"I don't know what game they're trying to play right now, but it just seems they're using our impact in tournaments, fining us and trying to benefit both ways from what we have to offer.

"In a way they're extorting players like myself and young players that have nothing to do with the politics of the game, so I don't like the situation and I'm not going to agree to that."

The eight players granted LIV releases - Hatton, Laurie Canter, Thomas Detry, Tom McKibbin, Adrian Meronk, Victor Perez, David Puig and Elvis Smylie - have all accepted that their presence at tournaments benefits the DP World Tour and its members.

Conditions for the players' release include paying outstanding fines in full for breaching the DP World Tour's regulations, as well as withdrawing all pending appeals.

The group must also participate in additional stipulated DP World Tour tournaments and media activity.

'I'll pay to play in Ryder Cup - but not to be a DP World Tour member'

Rory McIlroy previously said Rahm and Hatton should pay their fines and prove their commitment to Team Europe, after the furore of Team America being given money for their appearances at the 2025 tournament in New York.

McIlroy told reporters in January: "We went really hard on the Americans about being paid to play. We also said that we would pay to play in Ryder Cups. There's two guys that can prove it."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player In January, Rory McIlroy urged Jon Rahm and Hatton to pay their outstanding DP World Tour fines to prove how much it means to them to play for Europe in the Ryder Cup

On McIlroy's comments, Rahm added: "That statement would make a lot more sense if all 12 of us were being asked to pay, not only just the two of us.

"There's more intricacy that goes into this whole situation, right.

"While I understand why he's saying that, we all do it for the love of the game, it's a different situation than what we usually see.

"I'll gladly pay my way to go on the Ryder Cup, not have to pay to still be a member of the DP World Tour and fulfil a commitment that I'm fully willing to commit."
